summary: Cross-bead integration of nekh.1/.2/.3 on the merged spec-100 branch is clean. plan.go carries BOTH nekh.1 edits (normalizeImpactedDomains wiring at ValidatePlan L129-145 feeding `impacted` into both downstream gates; R2 hasAcceptedCitation + dual-remedy message in checkADRCoverage L547-583) AND nekh.3 edits (empty-citations WARN/ERROR at L159/L161 both naming the `adr_citations` frontmatter key). Neither merge clobbered the other — read ValidatePlan top-to-bottom and confirmed normalization runs before the citation/coverage gates, and the empty-citations branch is intact and uses the new key name. No interference: nekh.1 normalizes the DOMAIN set (OWNERSHIP.yaml path-glob resolution) while nekh.3 touches the ADR_CITATIONS list and scaffold key — orthogonal; the normalized `impacted` set only feeds checkADRCitations/checkADRCoverage by name, unchanged in shape. nekh.2's adr.go overlay (adrReadStore via FindLocalRoot + NewOverlayStore, CLI show/list only) reuses the same overlay primitive as the validator's adrStoreForSpec but constructs an independent read-store for the CLI; it reads ADR docs, not OWNERSHIP manifests, so it does not interact with nekh.1's ownership resolution. normalizeImpactedDomains has exactly two consistent call sites with the documented exec/ownerRef plumbing (divergence: exec/ownerRef ref-anchored; plan: nil/'' working-tree). No ordering dependency violated by the independent (no depends_on) merges — the shared helper lives in ownership_resolve.go (new file, nekh.1) and both consumers compile against it. `go build ./...` exits 0; targeted suite (TestValidatePlan|TestPlanCoverage|TestScaffoldPlan|TestNormalizeImpactedDomains|TestValidateDivergence) PASS on ./internal/validate/ and ./internal/approve/, with named tests verified to actually run (5 NormalizeImpactedDomains + ScaffoldPlanEmitsADRCitations confirmed PASS, not zero-matched).
